relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Troubled hearts are invited into trust.
The Father’s house has many dwelling places.
He prepares place so they may be where he is.
The way is already known in him.
Thomas asks the honest question of not knowing the way.
Jesus is way, truth, and life into the Father.
Knowing Jesus becomes knowing and seeing the Father.
Philip asks for the Father to be shown.
Seeing Jesus is seeing the Father’s self-disclosure.
The Father remains in him and works through him.
Trust the mutual indwelling, or at least the works.
Trust opens participation in his works.
Asking in his name glorifies the Father in the Son.
The name becomes the place of answered asking.
Love keeps commands.
Another Advocate is given to be with them into the age.
The Spirit of truth remains beside and within.
They will not be left orphaned.
Because he lives, they will live.
That day reveals mutual indwelling: Father, Son, and disciples.
Love, command-keeping, Father-love, and revelation interweave.
Judas not Iscariot asks why revelation is inward rather than world-display.
Love makes the human heart a dwelling place for Father and Son.
Refusal of love refuses the Father’s word.
These words are spoken while he remains with them.
The Advocate, holy Spirit, will teach and remind.
His peace is not world-peace; it steadies the heart without fear.
Love would rejoice in his return to the Father.
Foretelling becomes trust when it unfolds.
The ruler of the world comes but has nothing in him.
Love of the Father becomes obedient action: rise, let us go.
